name: Chocolate and Chestnut Loaf
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Darkest plain chocolate (at least 55% cocoa, 75% is great!)
recipeInstructions:
1. Recommended to make 1 day ahead.
2. Melt the chocolate until it is smooth and glossy.
3. In a large bowl, beat the butter until pale and creamy. Add sugar and continue beating until fluffy and light in texture.
4. Add the chestnut puree and beat until thoroughly blended and smooth. If using without a mixer, press the puree through a sieve first to ensure a lump-free mixture.
5. Stir in the melted chocolate, brandy, and 1 tablespoon water. Mix thoroughly.
6. Brush a 2-pound bread tin lightly with oil and line with greaseproof paper, brushing lightly with oil on top.
7. Pour the batter into the tin, flattening the top and covering with lightly oiled greaseproof paper.
8. Cover the tin with foil and refrigerate for at least 8 hours (or overnight).
9. Serve straight from the fridge, optionally decorated with piped cream and/or maron glace.
Note: This is a very rich dessert; a three-quarter inch slice is usually sufficient.
Time: Approximately one hour preparation plus eight hours chilling time.
